source_quote: Week 8 Systolic BP MPH ER 123 +/- 12, Placebo 125 +/- 12, Wilcoxon U-test P = 0.61
Week 8 Diastolic BP MPH ER 80 +/- 9, Placebo 83 +/- 8, Wilcoxon U-test P = 0.14

Baseline Systolic blood pressure OROS-MPH 117.8 (11.4), Placebo 115.4 (12.4). Treatment main effect F=12.8, P=.0004. OR for systolic prehypertension 3.32 (95% CI 1.41-8.37, P=.006) among baseline normal BP participants.

Baseline Diastolic blood pressure OROS-MPH 73.4 (7.4), Placebo 72.6 (8.0). Treatment main effect F=6.69, P=.010. OR for diastolic prehypertension 4.32 (95% CI 1.56-14.0, P=.004) among baseline normal BP participants.
At the double-blind visit, mean [SD] change in blood pressure from baseline was similar for placebo (systolic, 3.0 [8.52] mmHg) and MPH ERCT groups (systolic, 1.5 [8.95] mmHg).
At the double-blind visit, mean [SD] change in blood pressure from baseline was similar for placebo (diastolic, 2.4 [7.22] mmHg) and MPH ERCT groups (diastolic, 1.3 [7.05] mmHg).

No severe AEs or serious AEs were reported, and no deaths occurred at any time during the study. One subject assigned to placebo discontinued the study during the double-blind treatment period (lost to follow-up). No subjects withdrew due to adverse events during the double-blind period.
